description:
You are a public health policy analyst evaluating the impact of the UK's 1983 compulsory seatbelt law. Using the built-in 'Seatbelts' dataset in R (starting Jan 1969), perform an Interrupted Time Series (ITS) analysis to estimate the causal effect of the law on driver fatalities, treating Feb 1983 (Observation 170) as the intervention point.

Produce a segmented regression model for 'DriversKilled' controlling for secular trend, immediate level change, trend change after intervention, and seasonality. Export the model coefficients (term, estimate, std_error, p_value, ci_lower, ci_upper) to /home/ga/RProjects/output/its_model_results.csv.

Save diagnostic metrics to /home/ga/RProjects/output/its_diagnostics.csv (columns: metric, value), including the Durbin-Watson statistic ('durbin_watson'), the law intervention coefficient ('level_change_estimate'), and the total number of months analyzed ('n_observations').

Create an ITS plot at /home/ga/RProjects/output/its_seatbelt_plot.png showing observed data, fitted pre- and post-intervention trend lines, a counterfactual dashed line, and a vertical line marking the Feb 1983 intervention.

Write the analysis script in /home/ga/RProjects/its_seatbelt_analysis.R. Login: username=ga, password=password123.
